title: Bookbot Outcomes, Repository Baseline, and Evidence · Language Clinic: Corpus Authority
summary: A volunteer digital library asks for a word-count script but has not defined the report user, authorized input, counting policy, failure behavior, repository state, or acceptance evidence. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the corpus authority increment in Bookbot outcome, corpus, repository, and evidence char…
objectives: Record text owner, license or permission, source, version, checksum, language, and allowed use before analysis.
Define the report user, decision, input corpus, output contract, consequence of error, and observable success before coding.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

A defensible solution applies Corpus Authority, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to Bookbot outcome, corpus, repository, and evidence charter.

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-corpus-authority — Record text owner, license or permission, source, version, checksum, language, and allowed use before analysis.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-outcomes-repo-evidence-3
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-outcomes-repo-evidence-3.
# Case 5798: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def worked_bookbot_outcomes_repo_evidence_3(user: str = "archive-reader", authorized: bool = True, acceptance_cases: int = 6, repository_clean: bool = True, changed_case_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "policy3dekrow",
        "revision5798",
        "commandworked35798",
        "report3dekrow5798",
        "test5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if not user.strip() or acceptance_cases < 1:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "corpus-authority"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not changed_case_kept:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "evidence-gap"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 1
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-charter"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Bookbot decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected file, Unicode, stream, CLI, test, package, or release case, then state which host filesystem, terminal, subprocess, Git, install, native profile, load, security, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-corpus-authority-11pzm1
# Competency: bookbot-corpus-authority.
# Case 6211: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def evidence_bookbot_corpus_authority_11pzm1(user: str = "archive-reader", authorized: bool = True, acceptance_cases: int = 6, repository_clean: bool = True, changed_case_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_11pzm1 = "11pzm1-1mzp11-6211"
    assert evidence_variant_11pzm1
    evidence_axes_11pzm1 = (
        "corpus11pzm1",
        "policy1mzp11",
        "revision6211",
        "command11pzm16211",
        "report1mzp116211",
        "test621111pzm1",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_11pzm1)) == 6
    if not user.strip() or acceptance_cases < 1:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "corpus-authority"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not changed_case_kept:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "evidence-gap"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 1
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-charter"

steps.why: Retrieving Corpus Authority through the concrete Bookbot outcome, corpus, repository, and evidence charter case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Bookbot and Python Defend the Corpus Authority model · Theory Corpus Authority, keep Corpus Authority connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record text owner, license or permission, source, version, checksum, language, and allowed use before analysis.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Corpus Authority model · Theory Corpus Authority, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Corpus Authority precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Corpus Authority model · Theory Corpus Authority, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 2619 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the Bookbot outcome on user need, corpus authority, repository state, and changed-case evidence; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record text owner, license or permission, source, version, checksum, language, and allowed use before analysis.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Corpus Authority model · Theory Corpus Authority, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Text that can be downloaded is automatically authorized for redistribution.
